Airbus officially committed to the massive, multi-billion dollar development of the A3XX (which would later become the A380), a massive, double-deck superjumbo jet expressly designed to break Boeing's absolute monopoly on the highly lucrative 747 market. This massive corporate gamble was heavily predicated on the aggressive belief that future global air travel would rely entirely on massive, high-volume hub-to-hub routing.

In Andhra Pradesh, the state government fiercely battled an absolute, massive ecological catastrophe. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On April 15, 1989, a horrific, incredibly massive, and deeply traumatic human crush occurred at the Hillsborough Stadium in Sheffield, England, during an FA Cup semi-final match. The massive, fatal crowd surge, severely exacerbated by catastrophic police failures and poor stadium design, aggressively crushed fans against the steel fencing of the terraces. The incredibly devastating disaster resulted in the deeply tragic deaths of 97 Liverpool fans, fundamentally and aggressively revolutionizing massive stadium safety protocols and crowd management across the globe.

On April 15, 1452, Leonardo da Vinci, the absolute, quintessential Renaissance Polymath, was born in Vinci, Italy. His incredibly massive, brilliant intellect and aggressive, unquenchable curiosity heavily spanned anatomy, engineering, botany, and art. While globally revered for massive, iconic masterpieces like the *Mona Lisa* and *The Last Supper*, his heavily detailed, massive notebooks aggressively conceptualized helicopters, tanks, and solar power centuries before the requisite technology existed, securing his absolute legacy as one of human history's greatest geniuses.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On April 15, 1469 (Traditional Date), Guru Nanak Dev Ji, the massive, highly revered founder of Sikhism and the very first of the ten Sikh Gurus, was born in Rai Bhoi Ki Talwandi (present-day Nankana Sahib, Pakistan). His deeply profound, massive spiritual teachings heavily emphasized the absolute, fundamental equality of all humanity, fiercely rejecting the oppressive caste system and massive ritualism. His massive, transformative philosophy aggressively laid the absolute foundational bedrock of the Sikh religion and community across the subcontinent.

On April 15, 1919, following the massive, horrific atrocities of the Jallianwala Bagh massacre just two days prior, the British colonial administration aggressively and formally declared martial law across the entire Punjab region. The heavily draconian measures included massive, humiliating public floggings, the aggressive cutting of water and electricity supplies, and the horrific "crawling order" which forced Indians to crawl on their bellies down specific streets. This massive, brutal suppression definitively and absolutely destroyed any remaining Indian faith in British justice.
